text.title: Threads Basics — Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the Threads Basics methodology + two self-routing anchors. Each pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Post at minimum 3×/day; optimal 7-10×/day. Reply to the first wave of comments within the first hour of each post for algorithm boost.

rationale.source: Meta product comms; Threads engagement reports 2024-2026

rule.rationale: Threads rewards reply velocity, not just post volume. Accounts that post but do not reply plateau within weeks; replies drive reach more than the post itself.

rule.statement: Never cross-post X/Twitter content verbatim. Rewrite for Threads' conversational register (warmer, less ironic, no link-thread formatting).
rule.rationale: Twitter irony and thread formatting don't translate. Verbatim X content reads as out-of-place and suppresses engagement.

rule.statement: Agents MUST NOT auto-post via unofficial scheduling APIs. Drafts are ranked by the agent and posted manually by the operator within prime windows.
rule.rationale: No stable scheduling API exists at the start of 2026. Meta is aggressive about automation detection — unofficial schedulers get accounts flagged.

rule.statement: Drafts MUST be ≤500 characters; validate length programmatically after generation.

rationale.source: Threads UI constraints; LLM character-count reliability data

rule.rationale: LLMs are unreliable at character-count limits. Over-length posts fail silently or look unprofessional on Threads' compact format.

rule.statement: Forbidden phrases in every draft: "comment X for Y", "tag a friend", "follow for more". Drafts containing any banned phrase are rejected.
rule.rationale: These phrases are flagged by Meta and suppress reach. Explicitly prohibit them in every prompt.

rule.statement: Hook bigram rotation: no draft may reuse the same opener bigram ("Hot take", "Honest question", "Real talk") more than once per weekly content pack.
rule.rationale: Hook repetition reduces engagement. Enforce banned-bigram lists in the prompt to force variety across the pack.

rule.statement: Every draft MUST carry a content-pillar tag (Opinions / Value / Questions / Personal / Curated). Cap agent-generated posts at 50% of weekly volume — the other 50% comes from operator's lived context.
rule.rationale: Without pillar tags, agents drift toward whatever style won last week. The algorithm rewards authentic conversation, not publishing volume.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold (audience overlaps Instagram demographics, brand voice allows hot takes + personal stories, team can reply within first hour), execute the daily content-pack ideation + ship procedure from `04-procedure.xml`.
rule.rationale: Anchor for the decision tree's affirmative leaf.

rule.statement: If any Skip-If condition is present (no Instagram-overlapping audience, brand voice forbids conversational register, no reply capacity, heavily-regulated industry), do NOT apply this methodology. Route to `growth-linkedin-strategy` or `growth-twitter-x-growth`.
rule.rationale: Anchor for the decision tree's skip leaf.
